user_stories.role: As a traveler using the web application
user_stories.goal: I want to enter a destination, trip length, budget, interests and travel style and get a clear day-by-day itinerary
user_stories.benefit: so I can plan each day of my trip quickly and confidently
acceptance_criteria: A Flask API endpoint GET /health exists and returns HTTP 200 with a JSON status object
A Flask API endpoint POST /api/itinerary exists and accepts JSON payload with keys: destination, days, budget, interests, travel_style
Successful POST /api/itinerary returns HTTP 200 and a JSON object containing top-level keys: destination, days, budget, interests, travel_style, overview, itinerary, tips
The itinerary array length equals the days value provided in the request
Each itinerary day object includes keys: day (numeric), morning, afternoon, evening, budget_note
Activity titles and descriptions are human-readable strings (not raw dictionaries or [object Object]) and any activity dictionaries include readable fields such as title, description, cost
Estimated costs are numeric values adjusted by the provided budget (no low/medium/high strings)
Invalid or missing required input returns HTTP 400 with a JSON error object containing an error message

user_stories.goal: I want a stable, deterministic itinerary generation contract so automated tests and Docker deployment succeed
user_stories.benefit: so CI tests and graders can reliably validate outputs
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/itinerary behavior is deterministic for the same input (e.g., uses a stable algorithm or seed) so automated pytest checks pass

Itinerary generation expands each user interest into several concrete activities before assembling days (e.g., food -> breakfast street-food lane, local market tasting, signature restaurant meal, dessert and tea stop, evening snack street; outdoor -> lakefront walk, city park reset, scenic viewpoint, nature trail)

Activities are assigned to morning/afternoon/evening and rotate so the same generic fallback is not repeated each day; rotation should use destination, day number, time slot and user interests to vary items
Each day attempts a distinct theme or planning focus where possible (culture day, food day, outdoor day, shopping day, relaxation day)
Activity items can be returned as dictionaries, but include readable fields (title, description, cost) so the frontend can render them without displaying [object Object]

user_stories.goal: I want the website to collect form input, validate it, call the API, and render results using stable IDs and functions
user_stories.benefit: so the UI integrates with the backend contract and automated checks that look for specific elements/functions will pass
acceptance_criteria: Form elements use stable IDs: plannerForm, destination, days, budget, interests, travel_style, resultsContainer, daysContainer, formMessage, errorMessage
Frontend exposes the stable functions: collectFormData, validateFormData, setLoading, showError, renderItinerary, renderDay, renderActivity, formatActivityText

Frontend calls POST /api/itinerary with JSON containing destination, days, budget, interests, travel_style and handles a 200 response by calling renderItinerary to populate resultsContainer and daysContainer
Frontend handles HTTP 400 by showing the JSON error in errorMessage via showError
The frontend must not create an image preview area (image integration is handled later) and must render activities as readable text using formatActivityText so no [object Object] is shown

user_stories.role: As a product owner responsible for itinerary quality
user_stories.goal: I want itineraries that avoid visible repetition and provide realistic cost estimates and daily planning focus
user_stories.benefit: so users perceive value and the itineraries are usable for real planning
acceptance_criteria: No single fallback activity string is repeated across all days; morning/afternoon/evening items rotate and vary by destination, day number and interest
Estimated costs scale numerically with budget input (e.g., budget mapping produces numeric cost adjustments) and budget_note explains the adjustment per day
Each day's morning, afternoon and evening activities are distinct and drawn from the expanded interest activity pool so multi-day trips do not repeatedly show the same three activities
Tips and overview are human-readable strings that summarize the trip and provide at least 3 useful, non-redundant tips
Generated output is stable and testable: itinerary length equals days, activity fields and day structure follow the contract exactly so graders can validate programmatically
